summary: Started C decompilation for fighter utility code in ft_unknown_006 and menu character-select code in mncharsel, replacing large chunks of handwritten assembly with C, adding supporting headers and struct definitions, and registering the new object files in the build. The PR also expanded Fighter and HSD_GObjList layout knowledge needed by the matched code. Review focused on cleanup after initial matching: prefer ternary TRUE/FALSE or macros for boolean-return helpers, combine declarations with initializers where possible, use full include paths, avoid unused header declarations, and bracket remaining inline assembly with #pragma push/pop.
what_changed: Removed most of asm/melee/ft/ft_unknown_006.s, replacing many routines beginning at func_800877F8 with src/melee/ft/ft_unknown_006.c.

Added C implementations/prototypes for many fighter helper accessors and predicates, including input checks, item checks through func_8026B2B4 and itGetKind, parasol-status checks, flag accessors, velocity comparison logic, SFX adjustment helpers, and wrappers around audio calls.
Kept func_80087D0C partly supported with inline assembly after a C attempt, with review suggesting explicit #pragma push/pop boundaries around the asm block.
Added src/melee/ft/ft_unknown_006.h with prototypes for the newly decompiled fighter functions.
Updated Fighter layout in src/melee/ft/fighter.h by splitting filler around offsets 0x1A94, 0x1A98, and 0x1A9C and adding a flags union for x2220_flag.
Added HSD_GObjList to include/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h, including the x20_fighters field used to iterate fighter GObjs through lbl_804D782C.
Removed a chunk of asm/melee/mn/mncharsel.s and added src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c with func_8025BC20 and func_8025BD30.
Added mncharsel data structures and enums in src/melee/mn/mncharsel.h, including Mode, StageSelectMode, GameRules, PlayerInitData, StartMeleeRules, StartMeleeData, VsModeData, and CSSData.
Added src/melee/mn/mntext.h definitions for TextGlyphTexture, TextKerning, and SIS used by mncharsel text rendering logic.
Updated obj_files.mk so the newly added C translation units participate in the build.
smart_moves: Converted simple assembly leaf functions into direct field accessors/predicates, making offsets like input.x65C_heldInputs, x1974_heldItem, x221D_flag, x2227_flag, and SmashAttr fields searchable in C.
Introduced the HSD_GObjList struct specifically enough to express fighter list traversal as lbl_804D782C->x20_fighters and fighterObj->next instead of preserving raw offset loads.
Improved Fighter layout only where needed for matched code, preserving unknown naming while exposing x1A94, x1A98, x1A9C, and x2220_flag.flags/bits access.
For mncharsel, created focused headers for the data structures required by the two matched functions rather than leaving all menu state as raw pointers.
Used enums such as Mode_Time, Mode_Stock, Mode_Coin, and Mode_Bonus to make the character-select match rules easier to read without over-claiming unknown fields.
Accepted review cleanup quickly; the review history shows changes requested first and a later approval saying, "Looks good now. Thanks for the PR!"
decomp_lessons: When replacing many boolean-return assembly helpers, reviewers prefer concise expressions such as ternaries returning TRUE/FALSE or a macro rather than repeated if/else return 1/0 blocks.
Declaration style matters for readability in large initial decomp drops: combine declarations with initializers when that does not hurt matching.
If a function is not fully matched and must remain inline assembly, keep the asm island narrow and clearly delimited with #pragma push/pop.
Do not add prototypes or header declarations speculatively; reviewers explicitly asked whether an ft_unknown_006.h declaration was used anywhere.
Use project include style with full paths for nonlocal headers; review corrected mncharsel includes from local quotes and then from <src/melee/...> to <melee/mn/...>.
Header guards should end cleanly with a final newline after #endif; review supplied this for both mncharsel.h and mntext.h.
Matching C often requires small but reusable struct-layout advances in shared headers, such as adding a list struct in baselib or splitting Fighter filler fields.
naming_conventions: File name uses ft_unknown_006.c/h, while the PR title misspells it as ft_unkown_006.
Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as func_800877F8, func_80087D0C, func_8025BC20, and func_8025BD30.
Unknown struct fields kept offset-style names such as x1A94, x1A98, x1A9C, x2220_flag, and unk_x0 rather than invented semantic names.
Known or inferred enum constants in mncharsel use descriptive PascalCase prefixes, e.g. Mode_Time and StageSelectMode_Random.
Full include paths use angle brackets for project headers, e.g. <melee/mn/mncharsel.h> and <melee/mn/mntext.h>.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted hundreds of lines from ft_unknown_006.s and mncharsel.s while adding corresponding C files and obj_files.mk entries.
For simple bit tests, matched assembly loads/rlwinm patterns with Fighter bitfield access such as fp->x221D_flag.bits.b7 and fp->x2227_flag.bits.b5.
For HSD_GObj traversal, modeled the global list as HSD_GObjList and iterated x20_fighters via next instead of hard-coded offsets.
For numeric menu text, func_8025BC20 builds TextKerning digits from lbl_804D1124[0][41].kerning, clamps values above 9999, and controls leading zero rendering with a render_zeroes variable.
For mncharsel language/mode behavior, func_8025BD30 matches switch-heavy control flow over GameRules mode and branches on lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP.
For the hard fighter SFX routine func_80087D0C, the PR used a C version plus an inline asm fallback/section; review recommended surrounding this with #pragma push and #pragma pop.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Prefer ternary operators with TRUE/FALSE, or a macro, instead of repeated if/else returning 1 or 0.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Combine declarations with initializers in local-variable setup where possible.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned whether one piece of code still served a purpose, indicating cleanup of leftover matching scaffolding was expected.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested #pragma pop and #pragma push around the inline asm s32 func_80087D0C(Fighter* fighter, s32 arg1) block.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether a header declaration was used anywhere.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Use <full/path/header.h>; final suggested includes were <melee/mn/mncharsel.h> and <melee/mn/mntext.h>.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested a clean final #endif with newline.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested a clean final #endif with newline.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Initial review requested changes; final review approved with "Looks good now. Thanks for the PR!"

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further match ft_unknown_006 functions such as func_80087D0C.
Search for subsequent changes to HSD_GObjList or lbl_804D782C->x20_fighters to see if the list struct was refined.
Search later mncharsel PRs for CSSData, GameRules, and TextKerning field renames.
Check whether the reviewed unused ft_unknown_006.h declaration survived or was removed in the final merged diff.
